title: Dynamic Programming: Knapsack (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the student iterates items in the wrong direction, base the recurrence marks on whether they reference the correct neighbouring row consistently with their own loop direction.

This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is knapSack(double[] values, int[] volumes, int capacity); the Python function is knapSack(value, volume, capacity). Both are correct.

Both backward (i from n-1 to 0, referencing row i+1) and forward (i from 0 to n-1, referencing row i-1) DP formulations are acceptable. Mark the recurrence and return value relative to the student's chosen direction.
Each item can be used at most once (0/1 knapsack). Solutions that allow repeated use of items are incorrect.

rubric_points.name: DP table allocation
rubric_points.target: A 2D table of size (n+1) x (capacity+1) is allocated and initialised to 0.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No 2D table is allocated; only a 1D array or no data structure is used to store subproblem results.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A 2D table is present but has wrong dimensions (e.g., n x capacity instead of (n+1) x (capacity+1)), or the table is not initialised to 0 when required.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: A 2D table of size (n+1) x (capacity+1) is allocated and all cells are initialised to 0.

rubric_points.name: Loop structure: iterating items and capacities
rubric_points.target: An outer loop iterates over all n items and an inner loop iterates j from 0 to capacity inclusive, covering all subproblems.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No nested loops are present; only a single loop or no loop is used.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Nested loops are present but the inner loop does not cover all capacities from 0 to capacity (e.g., starts from 1 skipping j=0, or stops before capacity), or the outer loop does not process all n items.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Outer loop iterates over all n items (forward or backward) and inner loop iterates j from 0 (or 1) to capacity inclusive, covering all required subproblems.

rubric_points.name: Capacity feasibility check
rubric_points.target: For each item i and capacity j, the algorithm checks whether volumes[i] <= j before deciding to include the item.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No feasibility check is present; the algorithm attempts to include items regardless of whether they fit, potentially causing index errors or always taking items.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A feasibility check is present but uses the wrong comparison (e.g., volumes[i] < j instead of volumes[i] <= j, incorrectly excluding items that exactly fill the remaining capacity).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: volumes[i] <= j is checked before attempting to include item i, correctly allowing inclusion when the item exactly fits or fits with room to spare.

rubric_points.name: Recurrence relation
rubric_points.target: When item fits: Result[i][j] = max(values[i] + Result[prev][j - volumes[i]], Result[prev][j]). When item does not fit: Result[i][j] = Result[prev][j]. Here prev is i+1 (backward) or i-1 (forward).

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No recurrence is applied; cells are set to a constant or the values array is returned directly without referencing previously computed subproblems.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A max-based recurrence is present but incorrect: for example, only one branch (fit or no-fit) is handled, the wrong row is referenced (same row instead of previous, enabling unlimited use of items), or values[i] + Result[prev][j-volumes[i]] is missing the subtraction of volumes[i].

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: When item fits: Result[i][j] = max(values[i] + Result[prev][j - volumes[i]], Result[prev][j]). When item does not fit: Result[i][j] = Result[prev][j]. The previous row (i+1 for backward, i-1 for forward) is referenced consistently.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: The method returns the correct maximum value that fits within the given capacity for all valid inputs.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Method returns 0 or an incorrect value for most inputs.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct results for simple cases (e.g., single item, zero capacity) but fails for multi-item inputs or when items must be excluded due to capacity constraints.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns the correct maximum value for all valid inputs; result is read from the correct cell of the DP table (Result[0][capacity] for backward, Result[n][capacity] for forward).
